briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for July 19, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Intensification of Global Climate Disasters and Threats to Cultural Heritage
sections.content: Recently, climate disasters have been intensifying worldwide, with observed changes in species such as the temperature of Dokdo rising by 0.13 degrees Celsius annually and house mice becoming prevalent [2]. Napa Valley has experienced extreme cycles of drought and heavy rainfall [6], and in the UK, 42% of total deaths after the third heatwave were attributed to climate change [41]. The United States is also suffering from extreme weather events [39], and as Canadian wildfire smoke spread to the US, former President Trump threatened to impose tariffs [35], indicating that climate disasters are crossing borders and causing complex issues. The UN is considering listing heritage sites damaged by war and climate change as 'in danger' [42], and domestically, the Bangudae Petroglyphs in Ulsan are submerged for 38 days a year [34], demonstrating how the climate crisis threatens even humanity's cultural heritage.

sections.title: ⚠️ Domestic Climate Crisis Phenomena and Social Vulnerability
sections.content: Domestically, heatwaves have become a chronic climate disaster, with temperatures reaching 40 degrees Celsius, causing people to struggle with sleep and become more irritable [8, 9]. A Level 1 heatwave emergency was proactively declared in Jeju Island [36], and these extreme weather phenomena are even affecting daily life, such as changing office dress trends and bringing Bermuda shorts back into the spotlight [5, 10]. Climate change is also directly altering agricultural ecosystems, reducing the cultivation area for Jeju's specialty tangerines and increasing the cultivation of tropical fruits [37]. In response to recurring flood damage, basin-based management is being promoted, shifting 'from line to area' [7], but fundamental countermeasures for these disasters are even more urgently needed.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Policies and Citizen Participation Towards Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Gyeongsangbuk-do is fully committed to attracting the 'National Climate Science Institute,' aiming to secure a research hub for climate change response and a carbon-neutral think tank [1, 12, 13, 18, 19]. Specific efforts to achieve carbon neutrality are continuing in various local governments; Yangpyeong-gun conducted practical training and capacity-building education for carbon neutrality basic plan implementers [16, 21], and Suncheon City launched the carbon neutrality point system 'Carbon Moa Tantane' [20]. University students are also actively promoting the energy carbon neutrality point system as carbon neutrality evangelists [11, 14], and citizens of Siheung City are directly developing carbon neutrality solutions [15]. Daejin University's Carbon Neutrality Support Center is actively engaged in educational activities to raise overall societal awareness, including visiting military units to conduct climate safety education [17]. Kang Jeong-il, Chairman of the Safety and Construction Committee of the Integrated Special City Council, emphasized the diligent implementation of climate change response and citizen safety projects [3], demonstrating policy commitment.

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Policy Challenges
sections.content: The government plans to increase renewable energy capacity by more than six times its current level by 2040 [27], and positive movements are observed, such as the Sunshine Green Cooperative raising a cumulative 310 billion won to expand renewable energy investments [22]. Measures to increase the use of renewable energy for the eco-friendly transition of the semiconductor industry have been proposed [23], and 'Haetbit Ieum School' and Bokda Elementary School are implementing ecological transition education where students learn about and even produce renewable energy themselves [24, 28]. Mokpo National University has received an excellent paper award at the New and Renewable Energy Society [25], demonstrating research achievements, and the government plans to initiate improvements to the offshore wind power purchase agreement and fixed-price contract systems [29]. However, despite 11 trillion won in funding allocated for greenhouse gas reduction, concerns have been raised about inadequate performance management systems [4], and worries are growing that human resource issues in the renewable energy sector, unresolved for five years, are hindering the expansion of facilities [26, 30].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Response Trends and Challenges
sections.content: A new Environmental Performance Index has been released, highlighting sustainability performance and future challenges [40], and in some regions, climate change activists are being lauded as heroes by governors [43], indicating growing awareness of climate action. However, there are also instances, such as the rejection of a federal government grant request to complete a 54km 'missing link' of a 135km rail trail in northern New South Wales, Australia [44], which demonstrates that challenges in international climate infrastructure investment still remain. Thus, the international community faces various efforts to address climate change, alongside difficulties in policy implementation and securing funding.

briefing.closing: Through the climate-related news covered in today's briefing, we were able to confirm the increasing awareness of global threats, various ongoing response efforts both domestically and internationally, and the challenges that still need to be addressed. Responding to climate change is no longer an option but a necessity, and sustained attention and tangible action are more crucial than ever. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Tears of a 7,000-Year-Old 'Neolithic Canvas'...Could 'Three Sluice Gates' Be the Solution?
articles.original_title: 7000년 전 ‘신석기 캔버스’의 눈물…‘수문 3개’가 해법 될까
articles.description: The Ulsan Bangudae Petroglyphs face a conservation and drinking water dilemma. The installation of sluice gates is being pursued to reduce annual flooding days from 38 to 1. Eighty world heritage experts have identified 'climate change conservation' as a common concern. The 8th World Heritage Site Managers Forum, held in Busan, saw global experts visit the site on the 17th.

summary.phenomenon: The Ulsan Bangudae Petroglyphs are experiencing annual flooding for 38 days, and on the 17th, 80 world heritage site managers and experts from various countries conducted a field visit.
summary.outlook: The installation of three sluice gates is being pursued to reduce the annual flooding days of the Bangudae Petroglyphs from 38 to 1, which could serve as a solution to the common concern of preserving world heritage in the era of climate change.

articles.title: Trump Threatens 'Tariffs' as Canadian Wildfire Smoke Blankets U.S.
articles.original_title: 트럼프, 캐나다 산불 연기 미국 덮치자 ‘관세 부과’ 위협
articles.description: Experts assert that 'the impacts of climate change are not the sole responsibility of one nation.' On the 9th (local time), wildfires were observed spreading in Boston Bar, British Columbia, Canada. As powerful wildfires persist in Canada and their smoke extends into the U.S., former U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to impose tariffs on Canada, citing their inability to control the fires. Wildfires have become increasingly frequent recently due to climate change.

summary.phenomenon: On the 9th (local time), wildfires were spreading in Boston Bar, British Columbia, Canada, and the smoke from these fires extended into the United States.
summary.cause: Wildfires have become more frequent recently due to climate change.
summary.outlook: Former U.S. President Donald Trump warned he would raise tariffs on Canada, claiming they failed to control the wildfires, while experts pointed out that the impacts of climate change are not the responsibility of one nation alone.

articles.original_description: 전문가들 “기후변화 영향, 한 나라만의 책임 아니다”지난 9일(현지시간) 캐나다 브리티시컬럼비아주 보스턴바에서 산불이 확산하고 있다. AP연합뉴스캐나다에서 강력한 산불이 연일 이어지고 있다. 산불 연기가 미국으로 확산하자 도널드 트럼프 미국 대통령은 산불을 통제하지 못했다며 캐나다에 대한 관세를 인상하겠다고 경고했다. 최근 기후변화에 따라 산불이 더욱 잦아···

articles.title: Jeju Declares Heatwave Emergency Level 1...Strengthened Checks on Vulnerable Groups
articles.original_title: 제주, 폭염 비상 1단계···취약계층 점검 강화
articles.description: Jeju Province has declared Heatwave Emergency Level 1, activating its disaster and safety countermeasures headquarters as of 11:10 AM on the 19th. This proactive measure aims to prevent heat-related illnesses and mitigate damage to primary industries, despite heatwave warnings being issued in 5 out of the usual 6 areas required for this declaration.

summary.phenomenon: As of 11:10 AM on the 19th, Jeju Province's Disaster and Safety Countermeasures Headquarters declared Heatwave Emergency Level 1. This was a proactive response, as the declaration is typically made when heatwave warnings are issued in six areas within the province, but on this day, warnings were issued in only five areas.
summary.outlook: Following the declaration of Heatwave Emergency Level 1, Jeju Province is operating a response system to prevent heat-related illnesses and damage to primary industries, and plans to strengthen checks on vulnerable groups.

articles.original_description: 폭염 행동 요령. 제주도 제공제주도에 폭염 비상 1단계가 발령됐다.제주도는 19일 오전 11시10분을 기점으로 재난안전대책본부 폭염 비상 1단계를 발령하고 온열질환과 1차산업 피해를 막기 위한 대응 체계를 가동 중이라고 밝혔다.폭염 비상 1단계는 도내 6개 지역에 폭염경보가 발효되면 발령된다. 다면 이날 5개 지역에만 폭염경보가 발령됐음에도 선제적으로 대응···

articles.title: Jeju's Signature Tangerine Cultivation Area Shrinks While Tropical Fruits Expand: 'Why?'
articles.original_title: 제주 대표 특산품 감귤 재배 면적은 줄고 열대 과일은 늘고 “왜?”
articles.description: Tangerine cultivation in Jeju decreased from 25,000 ha (2000-2003) to 19,625 ha (2024), while other fruit cultivation increased from 523 ha (2016) to 930 ha (2024). This shift is attributed to an aging farming population, farm closures for quality improvement, and climate change. The trend shows a decline in Jeju's signature tangerine cultivation and an increase in tropical fruit farming.

summary.phenomenon: The tangerine cultivation area in Jeju decreased from 25,000 ha in 2000-2003 to 19,625 ha in 2024, while the cultivation area for other fruits increased from 523 ha in 2016 to 930 ha in 2024.
summary.cause: The causes for these changes include an aging farming population, farm closures due to quality improvement and development, and climate change.
summary.outlook: Not mentioned in the article

articles.original_description: 감귤 2000~2003년 2만5000㏊→2024년 1만9625㏊기타 과수 2016년 523㏊→2024년 930㏊ 증가농가 고령화, 품질 개선·개발 따른 폐원, 기후변화 원인제주의 감귤 따는 모습. 제주도 제공제주에서 대표 특산품인 감귤의 재배 면적은 감소하는 반면 열대 과일 재배는 늘어나고 있다.19일 제주도의 농축산식품 현황을 보면 제주지역 감귤 재배 면···
